presentation_title: Simulation Based Mastery Learning for Endoscopy Using the Endoscopy Training System
conference: SAGES 2017 - Panel: Meeting the ABS Mandate - The Program Director's Guide to the FEC Galaxy

frame_analyses.content: Title slide with SAGES logo and abstract presentation title
Disclosures slide detailing funding sources and conflicts of interest
Flexible Endoscopy Curriculum slide with FES certification requirements and failure rate statistics
Circular diagram showing practice/learning cycle with multiple endoscopy images
Endoscopy Training System (formerly SCOPE) equipment overview with complete ETS model
ETS Tasks slide showing straight model (A) with tasks 1-3 and body form model (B) with tasks 4-5
Hypothesis slide stating that SBML using ETS will result in higher FES pass rates
Methods - Training Goals table with expert mean times, time standards, allowable errors, and overtraining parameters for 5 ETS tasks
Methods - Demographics table showing 17 subjects (10 PGY1, 9 female, 13 right-hand dominant) with baseline endoscopy experience
Study design flowchart: Pre-training Assessment (n=17) → ETS SBML Training (n=14) → Post-training Assessment (n=15)
Results table showing 48 mean training trials, 5 mean training sessions, 71% achieved expert standard on all tasks, 0.57 correlation between tasks completed and FES score
Results - FES Score bar chart: Pre-training 50.5 (18% pass rate, n=3), Post-training 72.7 (100% pass rate), p<0.0001, effect size 2.4
Results - FES Tasks individual task scores pre/post training across all 5 tasks with statistical significance markers
Performance Benchmarks chart showing mean FES scores by total endoscopy experience (1-150, 151-300, >300) with pass rates 70%, 88%, 89%
Conclusions slide: SBML using ETS platform is feasible for novice learners

cannot_score: This is an educational conference presentation, not a trainee performance video. It contains no actual FLS task execution by a performer.
reason_for_classification: Video consists entirely of PowerPoint slides with static text, tables, charts, and diagrams. Contains no live or recorded performance of any FLS/MISTELS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). The ETS (Endoscopy Training System) is discussed as an educational platform but is distinct from FLS tasks.

teaching_content.presentation_focus: Simulation-based mastery learning (SBML) curriculum using Endoscopy Training System for novice endoscopy trainees
verbal_cues: Expert mean times and standards for 5 ETS endoscopy tasks
Allowable error thresholds and overtraining protocols
Study design: pre-training FES assessment → SBML via ETS → post-training FES assessment
key_findings: 71% of trainees achieved expert standard performance on all ETS tasks after SBML
FES passing rate improved from 18% pre-training to 100% post-training (p<0.0001, effect size 2.4)
Mean 48 training trials across 5 training sessions required to reach mastery
Significant improvement across all 5 individual FES tasks
Performance benchmarks show correlation between total endoscopy experience and FES scores
teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: N/A - presentation of educational research, not technical skill demonstration
teaching_content.study_design: Pre-post intervention with 17 PGY1 residents (14 completed training, 15 post-tested)
teaching_content.study_population_characteristics: 10/7 from two institutions, 9 female, 13 right-hand dominant, median glove size 7, minimal baseline endoscopy experience (0 median endoscopies)

return_summary: No FLS task score can be computed. This video is educational/instructional content presenting a research study on simulation-based mastery learning for endoscopy training. It is not a performance video requiring FLS scoring.
